programme.title: Washington Week With The Atlantic
programme.desc: Annie Linskey, The Wall Street Journal; Seung Min Kim, The Associated Press; Tyler Pager, The New York Times; Yvonne Wingett Sanchez, The Atlantic.

programme.title: Firing Line With Margaret Hoover
programme.desc: Actor and filmmaker Ben McKenzie unpacks his documentary "Everyone Is Lying to You for Money" and why he argues the crypto boom is built on hype, risk, and deception.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Actors Ted Danson, Mary Steenburgen and William H. Macy trace their nonconformist ancestors back to the Puritan establishment.

programme.title: Great Performances
programme.sub-title: Now Hear This: The Call of Istanbul
programme.desc: Exploring the layered sounds of Istanbul where empires met from European classical music to modern jazz.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação
Instituto Nacional de Cine y Artes Audiovisuales

programme.title: International Jazz Day From Abu Dhabi
programme.desc: Jazz and blues musicians from around the globe gather to celebrate the power of music; host Jeremy Irons.

programme.title: NEXT at the Kennedy Center
programme.desc: Jazz saxophonist and composer Joshua Redman joins vocalist Gabrielle Cavassa to perform songs from his album, "where are we," and explores the myths and realities of life in America.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: American Masters
programme.desc: Celebrating the life and career of actor Dick Van Dyke on his 100th birthday; featuring never-before-seen footage, photos and home movies that detail his legacy in show business.

rating.system: Instituto Nacional de Cine y Artes Audiovisuales
Dirección General de Radio, Televisión y Cinematografía

programme.title: Magic Moments -- The Best of '50s Pop (My Music Presents)
programme.desc: Phyllis McGuire, Pat Boone and Nick Clooney host a trip back to the 1950s with a mix of live performance and archival footage featuring classic moments from artists such as Perry Como, Debbie Reynolds and the McGuire Sisters.

programme.title: Mister Rogers' Neighborhood
programme.desc: How pulleys work; a rainy story in the Neighborhood of Make-Believe.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: Prunella the Packrat; What's in a Name?
programme.desc: Arthur tries to help Prunella with her pack rat habits; Binky finds out his real name.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: On their annual laundry day, the Kratt brothers disagree on whether blue oceans or green forests are better; Aviva demonstrates how oceans and forests work together.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: The Hunters try to help an injured bird so it can join its friends migrating south for the winter.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.sub-title: Lyla Can't Sleep; Hairdos and Hair Don'ts
programme.desc: Lyla struggles to sleep and tries to figure out what is wrong with her sleep routine; Lyla tries to do her own hairstyle.

programme.title: Carl the Collector
programme.sub-title: The Fake Mustache Collection; The Lint Dinosaur Collection
programme.desc: Nico is tired of being mistaken for her twin sister; Carl is upset when Nico breaks the Lint Dinosaur Monday rules and makes a Lint Monster instead.

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood
programme.sub-title: A Trip to the Enchanted Garden; A Trip to the Crayon Factory
programme.desc: Mom Tiger shows Daniel the strawberry patch; crayons are made at the factory.

programme.title: Phoebe & Jay
programme.desc: Phoebe and Jay learn how to sort items; Phoebe and Jay learn how to make food labels, so everyone can participate in the potluck.

programme.title: Sesame Street
programme.desc: Cinderella needs to tell time for the ball, but her clock is broken, so Nina, Abby and Rudy wonder what tool they can use to fix it.

Dirección General de Radio, Televisión y Cinematografía

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!
programme.sub-title: A Room of Our Own; Zeke's Fairy Village
programme.desc: Malik and Zadie can't agree on how tidy to keep their bedroom; Zeke's friends build houses for the fairies who have moved in.

programme.title: Relish
programme.desc: Jewish-style potato latkes; kimchi jjigae.

programme.title: America's Test Kitchen
programme.desc: Crab and shrimp stew; reviewing gyuto knives; fresh salmon burgers with Sriracha mayonnaise.

programme.title: Cook's Country
programme.desc: The barbecue traditions of Lockhart, Texas; cooking barbecued chuck roast; the origins of ranch dressing; preparing tomatillo and Bibb lettuce salad with tomatillo ranch.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.sub-title: On the Table Stat: South Asian Style
programme.desc: The Magic Bowl consists of veggies, grains and eggs; a homemade roti with a spicy red meat dip; turkey keema served with sweet potatoes, which then gets stuffed into peppers; the secrets of thickeners.

programme.title: Outside: Beyond the Lens
programme.desc: Jeff is invited inside the rarely-seen operations of the 144th Fighter Wing base in Fresno.

programme.title: In the Americas With David Yetman
programme.desc: The Mississippi, the largest river in North America, presents a challenge for the Army Corps of Engineers around New Orleans.

programme.title: Ireland With Michael
programme.desc: Journeying through Connemara's rugged coast; sailing to Inishbofin where castles, abbeys and island folklore come alive through music and storytelling.

programme.title: Rick Steves' Europe
programme.desc: A royal tour of London begins at Buckingham Palace; Houses of Parliament at Westminster; Churchill's underground war rooms; cruising the River Thames to the Tower of London.

programme.title: Samantha Brown's Places to Love
programme.desc: Exploring Arizona's Route 66; the Petrified Forest; Jack Rabbit Trading Post; Winslow's historic La Posada; Kingman's Route 66 Museum.

programme.title: Relish
programme.desc: Old-school techniques of stone-grinding and making the perfect sourdough; using Baker's Field Flour in a delicate tart.

programme.title: Christopher Kimball's Milk Street Television
programme.desc: Vietnamese chicken salad with sweet lime-garlic dressing; Greek bean salad; romaine with Manchego and smoky pepper dressing.

programme.title: Lidia's Kitchen
programme.desc: Winter panzanella; a mix of warm roasted vegetables tossed with bread and kale; crespelle bar; farina gnocchi.

programme.title: This Old House
programme.desc: A Walpole ranch is reimagined with an ADU for aging parents, a primary suite bump-out, smarter baths and better flow from garage to new mudroom and kitchen.

rating.system: Dirección General de Radio, Televisión y Cinematografía

programme.title: Ask This Old House
programme.desc: Basic tools and supplies needed to paint a room; compost versus disposal; how to run underground power from a house to a shed.

programme.title: Horizons From PBS News
programme.desc: Science, health, technology and environment experts and professionals address issues such as artificial intelligence, medicine and climate science.

programme.title: American Grown: My Job Depends on Ag
programme.desc: The future of the agriculture industry and stories of how it touches us all far beyond the table.

programme.title: Austin City Limits
programme.desc: Country Music Hall of Famer Tanya Tucker sings songs from "Sweet Western Sound"; Brittney Spencer performs songs from her debut "My Stupid Life.".

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When the author of a book that sparked a nationwide treasure hunt is found dead, Barnaby must unravel clues to find the killer among a group of dedicated seekers.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Father Brown investigates when the competitive atmosphere at a local dance school leads to a dance with death.

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: Papa Ray's Vintage Vinyl Roadshow
programme.desc: American music is heavily laced with the DNA of the blues.

programme.title: Riverdance 25th Anniversary Show
programme.desc: The reinvention of "Riverdance" immerses viewers into the energy and passion of Irish music and dance.

programme.desc: Fred and Mr. McFeely visit a recycling center.

programme.title: Wild Kratts: Our Blue and Green World
programme.desc: While doing their annual Laundry Day, the Kratt brothers disagree on what is better -- blue oceans or green forests.

programme.title: Carl the Collector
programme.desc: Carl can't wait for the ice cream festival; Carl is upset that he has to wear a cast on his broken wrist.

programme.sub-title: Daniel Plays T-ball; Max Plays at the Library
programme.desc: Daniel and his friends play T-ball and find that Henrietta is a dance teacher and a T-ball coach.

programme.title: Phoebe & Jay
programme.desc: Phoebe, Jay and Lupe practice their fire safety plan when the fire inspector visits; Phoebe and Jay buy juice from Mr. Aguilar.

programme.title: Inside California Education
programme.desc: Students explore Black history and culture in AP African American Studies; San Diego's BrewTech program; new art gallery; a nonprofit helps kids unlock reading skills by mastering high-frequency words.

programme.title: View Finders
programme.desc: Exploring Iceland's rugged and unique landscapes, from icebergs on black sand at Diamond Beach to a fresh lava field.

programme.title: Wild Foods
programme.desc: Members of the Cree Nation reintroduce the American buffalo; a new breed of cattle ranchers on Montana's high plains; conservationists in the Centennial Valley work to restore the Arctic grayling.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.sub-title: Campeche -- Tale of a Pirate City
programme.desc: Exploring Campeche, Mexico, a colonial city defined by its history of piracy; turkey panuchos; La Pigua features regional dishes pescado verde and grilled octopus.

programme.title: The Hidden Map
programme.desc: Filmmaker Ani Hovannisian travels across modern-day Turkey unearthing buried secrets and the hidden map; she uncovers the forbidden past of the Armenian Genocide and sheds light on the "hidden Armenians" still living in the shadows.

programme.title: Riverdance 25th Anniversary Show
programme.desc: The reinvention of "Riverdance" immerses viewers into the energy and passion of Irish music and dance.

programme.title: Grantchester on Masterpiece
programme.desc: When a student from a prestigious college is found dead, Will and Geordie must infiltrate the murky world of campus politics to find the killer; Leonard returns from Marrakech.

programme.title: Grantchester on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Will and Leonard witness a deadly hit-and-run in Grantchester; Geordie traces the car to a pair of decidedly dysfunctional brothers, whose house lies far off the beaten path; Will and Geordie both struggle as unwelcome guests look set to stay.

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: When tragedy strikes a local family, Dr. Turner's treatment comes under scrutiny; Trixie worries about the future of midwifery in Britain.

programme.title: Compass Points From PBS News
programme.desc: Experts analyze prominent international affairs topics and America's role in a shifting world.

programme.desc: An expectant father receives a life-threatening diagnosis; Trixie assists a female wrestler after a headlock goes wrong; Dr. Turner learns shocking news that implicates the future of the maternity house.

programme.title: The Forsytes on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Soames commissions a country house despite Irene's distress; as Louisa faces a fresh start, Jo decides his future; Ann reveals a dark secret.
